name: Cloudways Managed Cloud Hosting API
what_it_does: Managed cloud hosting platform with REST API — enabling PHP/WordPress/Laravel/Magento developers to provision and manage applications across multiple cloud providers (DigitalOcean, Linode, Vultr, AWS, GCP) via a unified interface, with automated server management, SSL, backups, team collaboration, and a REST API for application and server lifecycle automation.
use_cases: Provisioning cloud servers and PHP applications via Cloudways API for automated deployment
Managing multiple applications across cloud providers via Cloudways API for hosting fleet automation
Automating SSL certificate management via Cloudways API for multi-site management
Scaling server resources via Cloudways API for traffic-based auto-scaling workflows
Managing team access via Cloudways API for agency multi-client management
not_for: WordPress-only managed hosting at scale (WP Engine or Kinsta are WordPress-specialized)
Enterprise with strict compliance requirements (Cloudways is SMB/agency-focused)
Direct cloud provider management (Cloudways abstracts cloud; use provider APIs for advanced features)
best_when: A PHP/WordPress agency or developer needs flexible managed hosting across multiple cloud providers — where Cloudways abstracts server management while offering choice of underlying cloud (DigitalOcean, AWS, GCP, Vultr, Linode) and API for automation.
avoid_when: You need WordPress-only hosting with specialized features (WP Engine), strict compliance, or direct cloud provider API access.

auth.notes: OAuth2-like token endpoint: POST email + API key to get access_token. Access token used as Bearer in subsequent requests. Token expires — must refresh. API key from Cloudways Platform API settings. No standard OAuth2 flows. No fine-grained scopes. No webhooks documented. Access token has 60-minute expiry by default.

pricing.paid_tiers: DigitalOcean 1GB: $11/month; grows with cloud instance size; AWS/GCP: cloud provider cost + 10-20% Cloudways margin
pricing.notes: Maltese company (Valletta). Founded 2011. Acquired by DigitalOcean 2022 (significant acquisition). 85,000+ customers. PHP-first: WordPress, WooCommerce, Laravel, Magento, Drupal. Cloud choice: DigitalOcean, Vultr, Linode/Akamai, AWS, GCP. No cPanel — custom Cloudways console. Breeze cache plugin (WordPress). CloudwaysCDN. Team collaboration (permissions). Platform is a managed layer on top of cloud providers. API available since 2017. Agency-focused features: client transfer. Post-DigitalOcean acquisition: integration with DigitalOcean ecosystem growing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with token auth (email + API key → access token). Token expires (60-min). No webhooks. GDPR. Maltese company, DigitalOcean subsidiary. No SDK. Multi-cloud provider choice. PHP/WordPress focus.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Server/application reads idempotent by server ID/app ID. Server provisioning: check for existing before creating. SSL install not idempotent — verify cert state first. Scale operations not idempotent — check current size.
known_agent_gotchas: Non-standard auth: POST email + API key to get access_token (60-min expiry), then use as Bearer
Access token expires in 60 minutes — must implement token refresh in long-running agents
Acquired by DigitalOcean 2022 — API may evolve to integrate with DigitalOcean API patterns
No webhooks — must poll for server provisioning completion (can take 5-10 minutes)
Multi-cloud: different cloud providers have different instance types and capabilities — check provider when selecting
Agent (Cloudways automated backups/SSL agent) separate from REST API — don't confuse

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. Token-based auth (email + API key → short-lived access token). Maltese company under EU/Maltese law. DigitalOcean subsidiary security posture. Multi-cloud — data residency depends on chosen cloud provider region. No fine-grained API scopes. WordPress malware scanning available. SSL auto-management.
